title: Add summary-card visualization (KPI + sparkline + trend delta)
description:
Apple Health's highlight/summary card: large metric value with unit, mini sparkline below, and a trend indicator (▲ 12% vs. prior period). Renders as HTML (not canvas) for crisp typography, with inline SVG sparkline.

## Visual spec
- Category pill at top: colored text label (e.g., 'HEART' in red, 'ACTIVITY' in orange)
- Large number + small unit (e.g., '72 BPM' with BPM ~40% size)
- Sparkline: smooth line with subtle gradient fill under it, ~80px tall
- Trend row: arrow icon + percentage + 'vs last week' subtitle
- Optional secondary stat line: 'Range 58-110 BPM'
- Rounded card with subtle border/shadow, fits Obsidian theme variables

## Config keys
- type: summary-card
- metric: heart-rate | steps | sleep-duration | active-calories | hrv | blood-oxygen | respiratory-rate
- compareWindow: same-length prior period (default) | week | month
- width, height, from, to, last

## Files
- Create src/visualizations/summary-card.ts
- Register in src/visualizations/index.ts
- Pattern: follow src/visualizations/intro-stats.ts (the existing HTML renderer)

## Acceptance
- Renders category label in Apple's color palette for each metric
- Sparkline inline SVG, scales with card width
- Trend delta correct: hides when no prior-period data
- No canvas needed; pure HTML/SVG for sharp text rendering
- Multiple cards compose nicely in a dashboard grid
close_reason: Add summary-card (HTML/SVG) with category pill, sparkline, trend delta, range meta; HTML viz registry wired so multiple non-canvas types can register

title: Add bar-chart visualization (Apple's selected-day highlight bars)
description:
Clean Apple Health-style vertical bar chart used for steps, sleep duration, flights climbed, distance, etc. Selected/latest day highlighted in accent color, others muted.

## Visual spec
- Vertical rounded-top bars, consistent width with small gaps
- Latest/selected day rendered in full accent color
- Other days in muted/desaturated accent (~35% opacity)
- Optional horizontal dashed 'average' line across all bars
- X-axis labels: weekday initials (M T W T F S S) when <= 7 days, else dates every N
- Y-axis: right-aligned max value label, no full grid
- Large KPI above chart: total/avg + unit + date range subtitle (Apple style)

## Config keys
- type: bar-chart
- metric: steps | activeCalories | exerciseMinutes | distance | sleepHours | flightsClimbed
- goal (optional, draws a dashed goal line)
- showAverage (bool, default true)
- width, height, from, to, last

## Files
- Create src/visualizations/bar-chart.ts
- Register in src/visualizations/index.ts

## Acceptance
- Works for at least 5 different metrics via metric config
- Latest day visually distinct
- Tooltip shows metric, date, exact value
- Stats panel shows total, average, best day
close_reason: Add bar-chart: Apple-style selected-day highlight bars, 6 metrics, weekday-initial axis (<=7 days), optional goal line, dashed average

title: Add heart-range visualization (Apple's HR range chart)
description:
Apple Health's flagship heart rate chart: a vertical line per day spanning min-to-max BPM with a dot marking the average. Used for HR, resting HR, walking HR.

## Visual spec
- X axis: days (or hours if single-day)
- Y axis: BPM, auto-scaled with 20bpm padding
- Each day: thin vertical capsule from min to max (rounded ends), ~6-8px wide
- Average plotted as filled dot (~5px radius) on the capsule
- Optional resting-HR horizontal dashed line across chart
- Subtle gridlines every ~20 BPM
- Capsule color: red (#ff3b30) with gradient to lighter red at top

## Config keys
- type: heart-range
- metric: heart-rate | resting | walking (default heart-rate)
- width, height, from, to, last

## Files
- Create src/visualizations/heart-range.ts
- Register in src/visualizations/index.ts
- Pattern: follow src/visualizations/hrv-trend.ts for gridline/axis setup

## Acceptance
- Shows min/max range + avg dot per day
- Handles sparse data (days without HR samples render as gap)
- Hit region per day capsule shows min/avg/max tooltip
- Stats panel: overall min, avg, max across range
close_reason: Add heart-range: min/max capsule + avg dot per day, 20bpm gridlines, optional resting-HR dashed line, metric config (heart-rate/resting/walking)

title: Add activity-rings visualization (Apple's 3-ring Move/Exercise/Stand)
description:
Build the iconic Apple Health activity rings visualization: three concentric rings (Move/red, Exercise/green, Stand/blue) with progress arcs, center numbers, and percentage labels.

## Data requirements
- Move: active calories vs. goal (default 500 kcal or user-configurable)
- Exercise: exercise minutes vs. goal (default 30)
- Stand: stand hours vs. goal (default 12, falls back to step-based proxy if unavailable)

## Visual spec
- Three concentric rings with ~18px stroke width, rounded caps
- Ring colors match Apple: Move #fa114f, Exercise #92e82a, Stand #1eeaef
- Arc starts at -90deg (top), fills clockwise based on progress
- Completed rings overshoot with reduced opacity for excess
- Center shows day label + three goal lines like '534/500 CAL'
- Multiple-day input: render small multiples (ring grid) with date under each

## Config keys
- type: activity-rings
- moveGoal, exerciseGoal, standGoal (optional)
- width, height, from, to, last

## Files
- Create src/visualizations/activity-rings.ts
- Register in src/visualizations/index.ts
- Pattern: follow src/visualizations/vitals-rings.ts

## Acceptance
- Single-day mode shows one large ring set with stats below
- Multi-day mode shows small-multiple grid
- Hit regions per ring for tooltip (value, goal, percent)
- Respects theme colors but uses Apple's signature ring colors by default
close_reason: Add activity-rings with Apple Move/Exercise/Stand colors, single-day big + multi-day small-multiples, overshoot arcs, per-ring hit regions

title: Visual QA: render examples/apple-dashboard.md in Obsidian with real data
description:
The 8 Apple Health visualizations and the examples/apple-dashboard.md file shipped in 1.6.0 without a real-data eyeball pass. Load a vault with at least 30 days of Apple Health data, open apple-dashboard.md, and check every section for visual bugs.

## Checklist
- summary-card x4 renders sparkline + trend delta; Range line shows only where metric has min/max
- activity-rings: single-day mode displays center labels readably; multi-day grid fits without overflow
- heart-range: capsules + avg dots + optional resting-HR dashed line all aligned to gridlines
- bar-chart: latest-day bar highlighted; goal line visible when goal fits; average line labeled
- sleep-schedule: sunset/night/sunrise gradient reads well in both themes; goal bar anchored sensibly
- weekday-average: max bar highlighted; overall-mean dashed line labeled; weekStart ordering correct
- oxygen-range: warning zone shading visible; threshold line labeled
- trend-tile x3: arrow color matches 'higher is better' semantics per metric

## Acceptance
- File new bd tickets for any visual issues found
- Capture 1-2 screenshots in the 1.6.0 launch blurb

title: Create apple-dashboard.md example tying all Apple Health visualizations together
description:
A flagship example file at /examples/apple-dashboard.md that demonstrates a complete Apple Health-style dashboard using all the new Apple Health visualizations (activity-rings, heart-range, bar-chart, summary-card, sleep-schedule, weekday-average, oxygen-range, trend-tile).

## Layout
- Top row: summary-card x4 (heart rate, steps, sleep, active calories)
- Second row: activity-rings (left, large) + heart-range (right)
- Third row: bar-chart (steps, 7 days) + sleep-schedule (7 nights)
- Fourth row: weekday-average (steps) + oxygen-range
- Fifth row: trend-tile x3 (resting HR 90d, HRV 90d, active calories 90d)

## Acceptance
- File renders end-to-end with sample data
- Visual rhythm matches Apple Health's own Summary tab
- Short narrative text between sections explaining what each shows
- Depends on all 8 visualization tickets being complete (blocker list)
close_reason: Create examples/apple-dashboard.md: vertical feed of all 8 Apple-style viz (summary-card x4, activity-rings, heart-range, bar-chart, sleep-schedule, weekday-average, oxygen-range, trend-tile x3) with inter-section narratives

title: Add weekday-average visualization (bars by day-of-week)
description:
Apple Health's 'Averages by day of week' bar chart: seven bars (Sun-Sat or Mon-Sun) showing average value of a metric grouped by weekday. Overall average dashed line across all bars.

## Visual spec
- Seven vertical rounded bars labeled S M T W T F S
- Bars in accent color, weekend days slightly different tint
- Dashed horizontal line at overall mean
- Small label above each bar with exact value
- Large KPI: 'Average X across N weeks'
- Highlight the max bar subtly (full-saturation vs. muted)

## Config keys
- type: weekday-average
- metric: steps | activeCalories | exerciseMinutes | sleepHours | heartRate | hrv
- weekStart: monday | sunday (default monday)
- width, height, from, to, last

## Files
- Create src/visualizations/weekday-average.ts
- Register in src/visualizations/index.ts

## Acceptance
- Correctly buckets data into 7 weekday groups
- Requires at least 7 days of data; otherwise renders informative message
- Hit regions show weekday + average + sample count
- Overall mean line visible and labeled
close_reason: Add weekday-average: buckets days into 7 weekday groups, dashed mean line, max bar highlight, weekStart monday/sunday, 6 metrics

title: Add sleep-schedule visualization (horizontal bedtime-to-waketime bars)
description:
Apple Health's sleep overview: horizontal bars per day showing bedtime to wake time against a 24-hour clock axis, with nighttime shading.

## Visual spec
- Y axis: days (one row per night), most recent at top
- X axis: time of day, 6PM on left through 6AM on right (or configurable window)
- Night-dark band in the background (sunset to sunrise gradient)
- Each bar spans from bedtime to wake time, colored by sleep goal achievement
- Inner darker band marks 'in bed' vs. 'asleep' time if data is available
- Weekday label + date on left gutter
- Horizontal dashed line at user sleep goal time

## Config keys
- type: sleep-schedule
- sleepGoal (hours, default 8)
- windowStart / windowEnd (defaults '18:00' / '10:00')
- width, height, from, to, last

## Files
- Create src/visualizations/sleep-schedule.ts
- Register in src/visualizations/index.ts
- Pattern: reuse bedtime/wakeTime logic from src/visualizations/sleep-polar.ts

## Acceptance
- Handles bedtimes that cross midnight
- Shows goal line clearly
- Hit region per night shows bed/wake times, total duration
- Stats panel: average bedtime, average wake time, schedule consistency
close_reason: Add sleep-schedule: horizontal bedtime->wake bars, sunset/night/sunrise gradient background, in-bed vs asleep bands, goal line, consistency stat

title: bar-chart: goal line hidden when goal > 110% of max, but bars still rescale to goal
description:
In src/visualizations/bar-chart.ts, two code paths disagree when the user sets a goal much larger than the dataset's max.

1. Goal dashed line only drawn when `goal <= max * 1.1` (line ~184).
2. Y-axis scale uses `chartEffectiveMax = goal && goal > max ? goal : max` (line ~207).

Result: if you set goal: 10000 on a week where max daily steps is 5000, bars shrink to ~50% height (scaled to goal) but the 'goal 10000' reference line never renders — so the user sees short bars with no visual explanation.

## Fix
Either always draw the goal line when it participates in the y-axis, or keep the line hidden and don't rescale bars to an unreachable goal. The first option matches Apple Health's behaviour.

## Files
- src/visualizations/bar-chart.ts (goal line block + chartEffectiveMax)
close_reason: Unified bar-chart y-axis on chartEffectiveMax. Goal line now always rendered when goal participates in the y-axis scale; max label + avg line use the same denom so bars, reference lines, and axis label stay consistent.

title: Refactor heart-range + oxygen-range into shared range-chart primitive
description:
heart-range (src/visualizations/heart-range.ts) and oxygen-range (src/visualizations/oxygen-range.ts) share ~90% of their rendering logic: min/max capsule + avg dot, gridline/axis setup, x-axis date labels, stats strip. The only real differences are y-axis bounds, colors, warning zones, and metric extraction.

Extract a shared range-chart module (canvas helper or private renderer) and have both call it with their own MetricSpec. This was deferred in ticket obsidian-health-md-661 — do it now that both shipped in 1.6.0.

## Acceptance
- Shared primitive in src/visualizations/ (e.g. range-chart-core.ts) with a MetricSpec-like interface
- heart-range and oxygen-range become thin wrappers (<40 lines each)
- No visual regressions — snapshot behaviour unchanged
- Optional resting-HR overlay in heart-range still works via spec hook

title: Add trend-tile visualization (Apple Health Trends card)
description:
Apple Health 'Trends' tab card: compact tile showing whether a metric is trending up or down over a longer period (e.g., 90 days vs. prior 180), with arrow + percentage + short narrative.

## Visual spec
- Category pill (colored text) + metric name
- Arrow icon ↑ ↓ or → with color: green for positive, red for negative (semantic per metric — e.g., rising steps = green, rising resting HR = red)
- Large delta percentage + unit delta value
- One-line narrative ('Your walking pace is faster than it was 6 months ago.')
- Mini sparkline comparing the two periods (current solid, prior dashed)
- Optional 'consistency' dot indicator

## Config keys
- type: trend-tile
- metric: resting-heart-rate | hrv | steps | vo2max | walking-speed | sleep-duration | active-calories
- currentWindow (days, default 90)
- priorWindow (days, default 90, precedes currentWindow)
- width, height

## Files
- Create src/visualizations/trend-tile.ts
- Register in src/visualizations/index.ts
- HTML/SVG render pattern like summary-card

## Acceptance
- Correct directional coloring per metric semantics (a table of 'higher is better' vs 'lower is better' per metric)
- Renders nicely in dashboard grid alongside summary-card
- Narrative string handles zero/flat trends gracefully
close_reason: Add trend-tile: HTML/SVG tile with category pill, directional-semantic arrow (green for improvement per metric), current+prior sparkline, narrative, consistency dot

title: Add oxygen-range visualization (range bars for blood oxygen / generic vitals)
description:
Apple Health range-bar chart applied to blood oxygen and respiratory rate (same primitive as heart-range but with its own type for discoverability and metric-specific y-axis/coloring).

## Visual spec
- Reuses heart-range logic but for SpO2 / respiratory rate samples
- Y-axis: 90-100% for SpO2, 10-25 for respiratory rate
- Accent: blue for SpO2, teal for respiratory
- Range capsule per day with avg dot
- Warning zones shaded (e.g., <95% SpO2 light red band)

## Config keys
- type: oxygen-range
- metric: blood-oxygen | respiratory-rate (default blood-oxygen)
- width, height, from, to, last

## Files
- Create src/visualizations/oxygen-range.ts (or refactor heart-range into a generic range-chart module)
- Register in src/visualizations/index.ts

## Acceptance
- Correct y-axis bounds for each metric
- Warning-zone shading when values are out of healthy range
- Stats panel: min/avg/max over period

## Note
Consider refactoring into a shared range-chart primitive after heart-range and oxygen-range are both built — depends on heart-range being done first.
close_reason: Add oxygen-range: min/max capsule + avg dot for SpO2 or respiratory rate, warning-zone shading (<95% SpO2 / >20 brpm), dashed threshold line
